skillName: Find the nth term of a sequence
misconceptionSummary: Students identify the common difference but do not adjust correctly to build the nth-term expression.

steps.narration: Start by finding the common difference.
The sequence increases by three each time.
If the difference is three, the nth term will start with three n.
Now compare three n with the actual sequence to find the adjustment.

visuals.label: Difference gives the coefficient, then adjust to match term 1

steps.narration: So the nth term of the sequence is three n plus two.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: using the common difference alone as the nth term. The difference helps build the rule, but an adjustment is usually needed.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Three n gives the right step size, but you must still check whether it matches the first term and adjust if needed.
